name: ENCODE Project (Encyclopedia of DNA Elements)
organization: ENCODE Consortium (Lead: Zhiping Weng, Jill Moore at UMass Chan Medical School)

brain_coverage.cell_types_available: Multiple brain-derived cell types including primary neurons, glia, oligodendrocytes, astrocytes, differentiated neural cells from iPSCs
brain_coverage.developmental_stages: Prenatal (8 PCW), postnatal through adult (40 years)
brain_coverage.samples_note: Brain tissue and brain-derived primary cells across multiple studies in consortium

experiments.functional_validation: 90% of human cCREs functionally validated via STARR-seq, reporter assays, CRISPR, transgenic mouse assays

regulatory_relevance_explanation: ENCODE provides direct functional annotation of regulatory elements and comprehensive chromatin maps. Enables variant-to-regulatory-element mapping, cell-type-specific regulatory annotation, and GWAS fine-mapping. Essential for identifying which noncoding variants affect regulatory elements.

publication_key.title: An expanded registry of candidate cis-regulatory elements

related_publications.title: Expanded encyclopaedias of DNA elements in the human and mouse genomes
related_publications.description: ENCODE Phase 3, foundational for current atlas development

related_publications.title: Regulatory transposable elements in the encyclopedia of DNA elements
related_publications.description: Regulatory TEs as cis-regulatory elements in ENCODE

data_format: BED, BAM, BigWig, GTF, JSON (via API)
download_methods: SCREEN Portal (web interface, no download needed)
API access via ENCODE REST API

computational_requirements.memory: 16+ GB RAM for analysis of large datasets

integration_plan.phase_1: Identify all ENCODE brain-derived cell types and assays; completeness check
integration_plan.phase_2: Filter cCRE catalog for brain-specific regulatory elements; annotate by cell type
integration_plan.phase_3: Cross-reference with autism candidate genes and noncoding variants
integration_plan.phase_4: Build annotation pipeline for variant effect prediction using ENCODE profiles

atlas_outputs: Annotated catalog of autism noncoding variants with ENCODE regulatory evidence
Brain-specific variant impact predictions (high-confidence via STARR-seq/CRISPR validation)
technical_challenges: Cell type nomenclature standardization across datasets
Requires mapping to autism-relevant circuit nodes
